summary.oneLine: A fragrant, spicy broth-based soup built on aromatics and warm spices; commonly made with goat, fish, or chicken.
notes: Pepper soup spice blends vary by household (uda, ehuru, calabash nutmeg, grains of selim). This SSA baseline supports either a dedicated spice blend or a simplified pantry version.
This is a broth soup (not a stew). Keep it light and aromatic, not thick.
Nutrition depends heavily on protein choice (goat/fish/chicken) and oil use (often minimal).
Choose fish version only if fish allergen is not active.
Some spice blends may contain allergens depending on sourcing—apply household ingredient preferences.

ingredients.label: Goat meat (preferred traditional option)
notes: Goat pepper soup is classic. If using goat, simmer longer for tenderness.

ingredients.label: Fish (alternative)
notes: Add fish near the end to avoid overcooking; apply fish allergen rules.

ingredients.label: Scotch bonnet (or habanero)
notes: Adjust to taste; traditional pepper soup can be hot.

ingredients.label: Pepper soup spice blend
notes: If you have a dedicated pepper soup spice blend, use it. Otherwise use the simplified spice set below.

ingredients.label: Black pepper
notes: Pepper soup should be peppery; adjust to taste.

ingredients.label: Ground nutmeg (optional)
notes: Optional warming note if you lack pepper soup spice blend.

ingredients.label: Salt
notes: Start lower if using seasoning cube; adjust at end.

ingredients.label: Uziza leaf (optional)
notes: Optional traditional aromatic leaf; add near end if available.

ingredients.label: Fresh basil (optional)
notes: Common accessible substitute for aromatic finishing herbs.

steps.title: Choose protein path
steps.body: Choose ONE primary protein: goat (classic), chicken (faster), or fish (add late). This baseline supports all three; cook times differ.

steps.title: Build the broth base
steps.body: Add water to pot. Add chopped onion, garlic/ginger (optional), black pepper, thyme/bay leaf (optional), seasoning cube (optional), and salt (start conservative). Bring to a gentle boil.

steps.title: Simmer goat or chicken until tender (skip if fish-only)
steps.body: If using goat or chicken: add meat to pot and simmer until tender (goat 45–60 min; chicken 20–30 min). Skim foam as needed. If using pepper soup spice blend, add half now.

steps.title: Add heat and finish spices
steps.body: Add hot pepper (optional) whole or lightly crushed for controlled heat. Add remaining pepper soup spice blend (or optional nutmeg). Simmer 8–10 minutes to infuse. Taste and adjust salt/pepper.

hazards.message: Hot pepper can intensify while simmering—start small, taste, and adjust.

steps.title: Add fish near the end (if using fish)
steps.body: If using fish: add fish pieces during the last 6–10 minutes so they cook through without breaking apart. Keep the simmer gentle. Taste and adjust seasoning.

steps.title: Finish with herbs and rest
steps.body: Turn off heat. Add uziza leaf (optional) or basil (optional). Cover and rest 5 minutes. Serve hot.

suggestions: Serve as a brothy soup on its own, or with a small side of rice/yam depending on household preference.
Commonly enjoyed hot and spicy; provide extra pepper on the side if desired.

reheatNotes: Reheat gently. If fish was used, reheat carefully to avoid breaking it up.
Spice intensity can increase overnight; adjust with water/broth when reheating if needed.

substitutions.notes: This simplified set gives a decent baseline; dedicated pepper soup spice blend is more authentic.
Use available hot pepper; start small and adjust.

equipmentFallbacks.doInstead: Skim foam during simmer for a cleaner broth; straining is optional.

allergenConsiderations.notes: Choose goat or chicken version when fish allergen is active.

knownGood: Pepper soup should be brothy, aromatic, and pepper-forward (not thick).
Gentle simmer yields clear flavor and tender meat.
Fish (if used) must be added near the end to prevent overcooking and breakage.

commonIssues.likelyCause: Under-seasoned broth or insufficient spice infusion time
commonIssues.fix: Adjust salt carefully; add more black pepper/spice blend; simmer 10 minutes longer.

commonIssues.likelyCause: Too much hot pepper or simmered too long with pepper
commonIssues.fix: Remove whole pepper; dilute with water/broth; balance with a squeeze of lime.

commonIssues.likelyCause: Not simmered long enough or heat too high causing tightening
commonIssues.fix: Simmer longer at gentle heat; cover pot; use tender cuts next time.
